Home page/Resources/Help & FAQ

Help Center and FAQ

Centrally explain quotations, documents, materials, orders and after-sales related issues to reduce communication costs before customers submit requirements.

Quotation preparationFile formatOrder processAfter-sales support

FAQ

The content of the FAQ remains general instructions. Matters involving price, delivery date and quality acceptance shall be subject to the formal quotation, contract or documents confirmed by both parties.

What information is needed to submit a quotation?

It is recommended to provide 3D models, 2D drawings, materials, quantities, finishing processes, inspection requirements and usage instructions. The more complete the information, the more accurate the project assessment will be.

What model file formats are supported?

Common formats include STEP, IGES, STL, 3MF, etc. When it comes to assemblies or multi-part projects, it is recommended to also provide a structural description or zip package.

Can I quote if I don’t have the materials confirmed?

You can first provide the use, stress environment, temperature, appearance and cost targets, and the engineering team will make suggestions for candidate materials.

Is the quotation result equal to the final transaction price?

Online or preliminary quotations are used for quick evaluation, and the final price is subject to confirmation with complete drawings, workmanship, inspection, quantity, delivery date and contract terms.

How are changes and cancellations handled?

After the project enters different production stages, the impact of changes is different. It is recommended to contact the team as early as possible to confirm the scope of change, cost and delivery impact.

Review Checklist

The price is subject to the quotationDelivery date is not guaranteedDocument security expresses cautionClear boundaries after sales
